examples.title: Linear Search in Directory
examples.description: Demonstrates searching for a file in a linear list directory implementation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SearchDirectory(dirList, targetName)
BEGIN
    FOR i ← 0 TO dirList.length - 1 DO
        IF dirList[i].fileName = targetName THEN
            RETURN dirList[i]  // File found
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN NULL  // File not found
END PROCEDURE
// Time Complexity: O(n)
// Space Complexity: O(1)
examples.explanation: This procedure performs linear search through directory entries to find a file by name. In worst case, it may traverse all entries.

examples.title: Hash Table Directory Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of hash-based directory with collision handling using chaining
examples.explanation: Implements directory using hash table with chaining for collision resolution. Provides O(1) average-case lookup time.

examples.title: Directory Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode for common directory operations - create, delete, rename
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CreateFile(dir, filename, metadata)
BEGIN
    IF NOT SearchDirectory(dir, filename) THEN
        entry ← new DirectoryEntry(filename, metadata)
        Append entry to dir.list
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE  // File already exists
    END IF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE DeleteFile(dir, filename)
BEGIN
    entry ← SearchDirectory(dir, filename)
    IF entry ≠ NULL THEN
        Remove entry from dir.list
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE  // File not found
    END IF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE RenameFile(dir, oldName, newName)
BEGIN
    IF SearchDirectory(dir, newName) = NULL THEN
        entry ← SearchDirectory(dir, oldName)
        IF entry ≠ NULL THEN
            entry.filename ← newName
            RETURN TRUE
        END IF
    END IF
    RETURN FALSE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: These procedures demonstrate basic directory operations. Each operation checks for existence of files to prevent conflicts and maintain directory integrity.

examples.title: Inode Structure Representation
examples.description: C structure representing inode with direct and indirect block pointers
examples.explanation: This inode structure shows how UNIX file systems store file metadata and pointers to data blocks. Direct pointers handle small files efficiently, while indirect pointers enable support for very large files.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for small files, O(log n) for large
